title: States Plow Ahead With A.I. Regulation, Defying Trump
description: The battle over who should regulate artificial intelligence is turning into an epic clash between President Trump and states like California and Utah.
content: This month, President Trump warned states not to get involved in regulating artificial intelligence. In a set of policy guidelines, the White House said a “patchwork of conflicting state laws would undermine American innovation and our ability to lea... [1797 chars]

title: Yahoo turns to AI-powered answer engine Scout to lead it back to its roots in online search
description: Internet trailblazer Yahoo is exploring technology's next frontier with Scout, an answer engine powered by artificial intelligence. Scout seems insightful, based on its response to a question posed by The Associated Press about why one of Silicon Valley's brightest stars faded away a decade ago. “Yahoo’s journey illustrates how a…

title: Quinnipiac Poll: 80% Concerned About AI
description: A new national poll shows growing skepticism about the impact of artificial intelligence.
content:
Americans are rapidly adopting artificial intelligence tools in their daily lives, but a new national poll shows growing skepticism about the technology's impact, with majorities expressing concern about jobs, trust, and regulation.
The Quinnipiac Un... [2582 chars]

title: Data centres' alarming impact on the environment: scientists
description: The vast data centres that power artificial intelligence guzzle huge amounts of energy but they also have another alarming impact, according to new research. They are creating “heat islands,” warming the land around them by up to 16 degrees Fahrenheit, and making life hotter for more than 340 million people.

title: AI eye exams accurately identify heart disease risk during routine visits
description: A new system that uses artificial intelligence (AI) to assess cardiovascular risk based on images of the eye captured during eye exams demonstrated strong correlation with a standard cardiovascular risk assessment, according to a study presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session (ACC.26).

title: Data centers are creating ‘heat islands’ and warming the land around them by up to 16 degrees
description: The vast data centers that power artificial intelligence are so energy hungry that they’re heating up their surroundings, according to new research. It’s an alarming finding given the number of data centers is predicted to explode over the next few years.
content: The vast data centers that power artificial intelligence guzzle huge amounts of energy but they also have another alarming impact, according to new research. They are creating “heat islands,” warming the land around them by up to 16 degrees Fahrenhei... [3126 chars]

title: AI tools are widely used by federal judges, study finds
description: A new Northwestern study surveying federal judges across the U.S. on their use and outlook on artificial intelligence in and outside of the courtroom found that more than 60% of judges who responded reported using at least one AI tool in their judicial work. While judges reported broad adoption of AI tools, only 22.4% of judges reported using AI tools on a weekly or daily basis.

title: UK Watchdog Issues First Guidance on Generative AI in Audit
description: The UK’s audit watchdog published guidance Monday on the use of certain artificial intelligence tools in reviews of corporate financial reporting, in what it called a world-first for a regulator.
content:
The UK’s audit watchdog published guidance Monday on the use of certain artificial intelligence tools in reviews of corporate financial reporting, in what it called a world-first for a regulator.

title: Meta, Amazon and Google blame artificial intelligence for massive job cuts: Is AI the real culprit behind tech layoffs?
description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has now become tech companies' go-to word for massive job cuts. According to a tech investor, blaming AI is better than citing cost pressure, since it makes the technology look bad. Major tech giants such as Meta, Amazon and Google announced job cuts in the past.

title: HPU Asst Prof to visit Japanese varsities on academic fellowship
description: As part of an international initiative focused on photonics and artificial intelligence, Tarun Sharma, an Assistant Professor at the University Institute of Technology (UIT) of Himachal Pradesh University (HPU), has been invited to serve as a visiting professor in several leading universities of Japan.
